creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
Best banh mi in Copenhagen? 🇩🇰 More info 👇  District Tonkin has been around for quite a few years, and I think it’s an excellent no-thrill place where you can get a pretty cheap (for Copenhagen) bite to eat. They have restaurants in Vesterbro, Nørrebro and right next to the Marble Church near a lot of the main tourist attractions in CPH 🎠 

I had the BBQ Pork bahn mi, which cost 84 DKK (€11,27 / $12.37). The fried pork wontons were 60 DKK for 3 pieces. That’s excellent value for money in Copenhagen, in my opinion 🤓

I’ve also had their caramelised pork with a boiled egg on a previous visit, and that’s also a dish I highly recommend ✌️

This is not a sponsored post - I just share restaurants and things to do in Copenhagen that I enjoy. For more Copenhagen recommendations, check out my travel blog and Youtube channel (there’s a link in my profile).

Follow for more Copenhagen content 🥳  #copenhagen #copenhagenfood #copenhagentravel

transcription.text: Trying the viral banh mi in Copenhagen. District Tongking is located right here in Vesterborg, which is actually one of my favorite parts of Copenhagen. And even though we were there for the banh mi, I just had to try these wontons and they were just perfect. Crispy on the outside, juicy on the inside. I definitely should have ordered more. Then it was time for the crispy barbecue banh mi filled with pork, cilantro, chili and cucumber. My girlfriend's baguette did kind of fall apart, but to be fair, I can't take her anywhere. So in conclusion, this was a great meal for a pretty cheap price and a solid 8.5 out of 10 from me.
